name: Livestock treatment and animal pain - Share of respondents that answered 'Somewhat agree'
descriptionShort: Weighted share of survey respondents selecting a response.

presentation.titlePublic: Livestock treatment and animal pain - Share of respondents that answered 'Somewhat agree'

descriptionKey: The survey has been repeated in 2017, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, and 2025, with 7,165 participants across six waves. Responses are weighted to be representative of the US population.

The questions related to livestock treatment and animal pain are:
- "Farmed animals have roughly the same ability to feel pain and discomfort as humans."
- "Farmed animals have substantially less ability to feel pain and discomfort than humans."
- "I am comfortable with the way animals are used in the food industry."
- "I have some discomfort with the way animals are used in the food industry."
- "Most farmed animals are treated well. For example, the animals are given enough space and kept in good health."
- "The animal-based foods I purchase (meat, dairy, and/or eggs) usually come from animals that are treated humanely. For example, the animals are given enough space and kept in good health."
- "The factory farming of animals is one of the most important social issues in the world today."

values.name: Factory farming is one of the most important social problems
Farmed animals feel substantially less pain than humans
Farmed animals have roughly the same ability to feel pain as humans
I am comfortable with how animals are used in the food industry
I have some discomfort with the way animals are used in the food industry
Most farmed animals are treated well
The meat, dairy, and eggs I purchase usually come from animals treated humanely

origins.title: Animals, Food, and Technology
origins.description: This data is from a cross-sectional survey on attitudes towards animals, food, and technology run by the [Sentience Institute](https://www.sentienceinstitute.org/). Each sample was recruited in partnership with [Ipsos](https://www.ipsos.com/) based on census estimates from the [American Community Survey](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs). The survey has been conducted, with minor changes, in 2017, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, and 2025, with 7165 participants across the six survey waves. The survey and analysis code associated with this dataset can be found at [OSF](https://osf.io/a4qjg/).
origins.citationFull: Sentience Institute. Anthis, Jacy Reese; Ladak, Ali (2025), “Animals, Food, and Technology (AFT) Survey 2017-2021”, Mendeley Data, V2, doi: 10.17632/k3xcndsswk.2
